description: Evaluate product artifact generation quality and completeness across all artifact types

scenarios.query: Create a PRD for adding dark mode to the application
expected_behaviors: Generates problem statement with user pain points (not solution-first)
Includes at least 3 user stories with proper format and specific personas
Defines measurable success metrics with baselines and targets
Lists acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format
Identifies technical requirements and dependencies
Runs validation script before completing
Applies product thinking checklist
Checks flywheel learnings before drafting
quality_gates: validate_artifact.py returns exit code 0
Problem statement > 100 characters and describes user pain
User stories >= 3 with specific personas (not 'as a user')
Acceptance criteria >= 5 and machine-testable
Success metrics have numeric targets with baselines
Health-check metric paired with each primary metric
Risks include at least one user-behavior risk

scenarios.query: Create a PRD for real-time collaboration features like Google Docs
expected_behaviors: Addresses technical complexity in requirements
Includes scalability considerations
Defines offline sync behavior
Identifies risks related to conflict resolution
Proposes phased rollout approach
Applies context adaptation for platform product type
References relevant frameworks (MVP spectrum for phasing)
quality_gates: Technical requirements section present
Risks section has >= 3 identified risks including user-behavior risks
Out of scope section clearly defines boundaries with genuinely tempting items
Timeline includes beta/staged rollout
Acceptance criteria address offline and mobile scenarios

scenarios.query: Create a PRD for GDPR data export functionality
expected_behaviors: References specific regulatory requirements
Includes compliance-focused acceptance criteria
Defines data retention and deletion requirements
Addresses audit logging needs
Considers performance for large data exports
Applies context adaptation for enterprise signal
quality_gates: Mentions specific regulation articles/sections
Includes security considerations
Defines maximum export time SLA
Audit log requirements specified
Compliance gate included in any launch readiness reference

scenarios.query: Create a competitive analysis for our AI code assistant tool in the developer tools market
expected_behaviors: Identifies at least 4 competitors with detailed profiles
Creates feature-by-feature differentiation matrix
Includes pricing model comparison
Analyzes recent competitive moves (funding, launches, pivots)
Provides strategic implications specific to our positioning
Recommends concrete responses ranked by urgency
Assesses moat durability for each competitive advantage
Applies context adaptation for growth stage and B2B signals
References relevant frameworks (Value Proposition Canvas for differentiation)
quality_gates: Competitors >= 3 with positioning, strengths (>=2), weaknesses (>=2)
Differentiation matrix is a complete table, not a list
Pricing models documented for each competitor
Strategic implications >= 3 with specific roadmap actions
Moat assessment distinguishes durable vs temporary advantages
Recent moves section covers last 12 months
Recommended responses are prioritized, not just listed

scenarios.query: Create a RICE prioritization matrix for our Q2 roadmap candidates: SSO integration, mobile app redesign, API v2, in-app analytics dashboard, workflow automation builder, and CSV bulk import improvements

context.users: 5,000 active workspaces, mix of SMB and mid-market

data_available.feature_requests: SSO: 45 requests, Mobile: 120, API v2: 30, Analytics: 85, Automation: 60, CSV: 25
data_available.churn_mentions: SSO: 12 churned accounts cited it, Mobile: 5, Analytics: 8
data_available.revenue_impact: SSO blocks $800K pipeline, API v2 blocks $200K partner deals

expected_behaviors: Applies RICE framework correctly with all four dimensions
Scores each item with explicit justification per dimension
Uses the provided data to inform reach and impact scores
Identifies a clear cut line with reasoning
Documents assumptions that could change the ranking
Performs sensitivity analysis on top-ranked items
Flags items where confidence is low and recommends data gathering
Detects heuristic patterns (e.g., SSO as enterprise signal, mobile requests without retention data)
Checks for anti-patterns (feature parity, HiPPO influence)
quality_gates: All 6 items scored across Reach, Impact, Confidence, Effort
Each score has a justification (not just a number)
Final ranking is internally consistent with the scores
Cut line is explicit with reasoning for the boundary
Assumptions section lists >= 3 key assumptions
Sensitivity analysis identifies items that would change rank with 20% score shifts
At least one item flagged for low confidence with recommendation to gather more data
Evidence from context (feature requests, churn, revenue) is referenced in scoring

scenarios.query: Create a launch readiness checklist for our new team permissions system that adds role-based access control (RBAC) to the platform

context.feature: RBAC with custom roles, permission inheritance, and audit logging
context.users_affected: All 5,000 active workspaces will be migrated
risks: Existing permissions may change for some users during migration
Custom integrations via API may break if permission checks change
Support team has never handled RBAC-related tickets

expected_behaviors: Covers all required gate domains: product quality, engineering stability, support readiness, marketing, legal/compliance, analytics
Each gate has status, owner, evidence, and blockers
Addresses migration-specific risks (data migration, backward compatibility)
Includes rollback criteria and plan
Defines post-launch monitoring schedule
Includes communication plan for affected users
Addresses API backward compatibility for integrations
Applies context adaptation for enterprise and B2B signals
References cross-agent handoffs (engineering for stability, support for readiness, marketing for comms)
quality_gates: All 6 gate domains present with at least 2 items each
Every gate item has status (green/yellow/red), owner, and evidence
Go/no-go recommendation present with risk summary
Rollback criteria are specific and measurable (not 'if things go wrong')
Post-launch plan includes first review date and success criteria
Migration plan addresses existing user permissions
API backward compatibility addressed explicitly
Support readiness includes FAQ, escalation paths, and team briefing status
Analytics gate confirms tracking is implemented and dashboards are live

common_failures: No consideration of product stage or context
No reference to prior learnings or decisions
Competitive analysis is a feature list without strategic implications
Prioritization is gut-feel without a framework
Launch checklist misses support readiness and analytics

key_improvements: Structured output following template for all artifact types
Measurable metrics with baselines and health-check pairs
Context adaptation protocol adjusts artifact depth and focus
Flywheel learnings checked before drafting and submitted after
Frameworks applied appropriately, not just referenced
